raw.objective: To model the changing catabolic enzyme activity, linearly related to hepatic microsomal enzyme concentration, along with the serum drug concentration.
raw.observations: An atypical one compartment model, using complex methods to take into account the induction and its plateau of CBZ clearance. Of main interest is the finding that autoinduction is maximised above a daily dose of about 500 mg, The unusual type of model doesn’t easily allow the application in practice. The absence of ipred vs obs concentrations doesn’t allow to assess the fit of the model, although the Bayesian predictions do to some extent
raw.structure: 1 compartment, linear absorption and elimination with non-linear autoinduction elements
raw.equations: Kind=theta1 CL0=theta2*WT Cdouble=theta3 Indmax=theta4 Vd=theta5*WT F=theta6 CLt+dt=CLt+dt*Kind*((1+(Ct*Indmax)/(Ct+Cdouble*(Indmax-1)))*CL0-CLt) Ct+dt=Ct+dt*((FD-CLt*Ct)/Vd) F=bioavailability D=dose rate (mg/kg/h) during interval dt Ct=concentration at time t dt=delta time CLt=clearance at time t Kind=induction constant Cdouble=concentration at which induction is maximal CL0=clearance before induction
raw.parameter_values: theta1=0.00963 theta2=0.021 theta3=7.5 theta4=5 theta5=1.1 theta6=0.9 Variances not reported (“rule of thumb”)

structural_model.description: 1 compartment, linear absorption and elimination with non-linear autoinduction elements

extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.objective: To model the changing catabolic enzyme activity, linearly related to hepatic microsomal enzyme concentration, along with the serum drug concentration.

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
